alias.description: Creates a shortcut for a command. Aliases are expanded before execution.

function.description: Defines a shell function. Functions can accept arguments accessed via $1, $2, etc.

export.description: Sets and exports an environment variable, making it available to child processes.

source.description: Read and execute commands from a file in the current shell environment. Also: . file

setopt.description: Enable a zsh shell option. Use unsetopt to disable.

autoload.description: Mark a function for autoloading. -U suppresses alias expansion, -z forces zsh-style.

bindkey.description: Bind a key sequence to a ZLE (Zsh Line Editor) widget.

zstyle.description: Set context-sensitive style for the completion system and other modules.

compdef.description: Associate a completion function with a command.

PROMPT.description: The primary prompt string. Supports escape sequences: %n (username), %m (hostname), %~ (cwd), %F{color}...%f.

RPROMPT.description: Right-side prompt. Disappears when typing reaches it. Useful for git status, time, etc.

HISTFILE.description: File where command history is saved.

HISTSIZE.description: Maximum number of history entries kept in memory.

SAVEHIST.description: Maximum number of history entries saved to HISTFILE.

ZSH_THEME.description: Oh My Zsh theme selector. Set before sourcing oh-my-zsh.sh.

AUTO_CD.description: If a command is a directory name, cd into it automatically.

GLOB_DOTS.description: Include dotfiles in glob patterns without explicitly specifying the dot.

SHARE_HISTORY.description: Share history between all running zsh sessions.
